channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on street cabinet, 12th July
item.description:
Street cabinet on south west side of junction of Bridgewood close with Casteldine Road Blue and black tags, depicting OOPS on green painted metal surface of a street cabinet

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike louts dumping bikes when a rack was available, 12th July
item.description:
Charlton Village: 9.02am Sunday 12 July. Two male Lime bike riders travelling from west to east along Charlton Road veered off at the junction with Church Lane, rode on both the wrong side of the road and on the pavement and then dumped their bikes around the war memorial. When it was pointed out that there was an empty bike rack across the road the response was that it was too dangerous for them to cross the road. If road conditions are so dangerous, then why were they cycling at all? This was an “Up yours, we’ll do as we like” response from two entitled louts with no thought for pedestrians or their fellow cyclists who obey the law. Lime ought to be able to trace this pair from their log-off time/location but are unlikely to take action as the watchword is “profit”. No wonder there is so much anger towards hire bike users who get away with causing serious injury. According to the BBC, Jane Ouartsi was walking across an empty St Giles Square in central London on 4 August 2023 when she was hit by a Lime bike. She suffered a broken femur, a fractured collar bone and two spinal fractures requiring three operations and spent 36 days in hospital but there has been no compensation from Lime. You should all be ashamed…. if only!

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green Lane, Penge, area filthy with litter and rubbish, 12th July
item.description:
See photo for area that needs attention. Paved seating area next to railway bridge, around bench, within shrub area to left of and behind bench, who area needs thorough litterpick, with thorough attention need within and under shrubs. Please can this be completed properly the first time of asking. Https://w3w.co/jabs.passes.cups

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Building waste fly tipped under tree, 12th July
item.description:
Outside 15/16 Bridgewood Close, under tree on right hand side of car park. One pot of tile adhesive and two bags of other building waste.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bridgewood Close, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Various litter bins full and overflowing, 12th July

item.title: Graffiti on street cabinet and postal box, 12th July
item.description:
On the street cabinet and postal box, found at the gable end of 135 Anerley Road, at the junction with Ridsdale Road... One tag depicting LUNA in yellow on green painted metal of street cabinet And One tag depicting KLEAN in white on black postal cabinet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 135, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Brambles, 12th July
item.description:
Several large brambles growing over from the front garden of the property, causing a hazard to people walking by.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnell Avenue, East Wickham, Greater London

item.title: Bycroft St, Penge, tatty with litter, 12th July
item.description:
Street needs litterpicking please, including seating area, raised grass area. Area looking neglected currently so would appreciate this been completed the first time of asking.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bycroft Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on Park End Bromley, 12th July
item.description:
2 mattresses and a bed frame have been left on the pavement on the corner of Park End. They are an eyesore and obstructing the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park End, Plaistow, Greater London

item.title: By railway Claremont road, 12th July
item.description:
Large bag and shoes dumped on corner we need cctv here to monitor deterrent

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Claremont Road, London
